# CRM cross-dialect test fixture

A 14-table CRM schema in three native dialects (MSSQL, PostgreSQL, MySQL)
plus a runner that drives uvg through every (source, target) permutation
and reports apply success. Strict matrix runs also verify MSSQL→PostgreSQL
column metadata fidelity.

This is the fixture uvg's translation tables were validated against. As of
v1.5.0, all 9 permutations pass cleanly — every cross-dialect run lands
14 tables, 22 foreign keys, 24-36 CHECK constraints, and 13-28 indexes,
end-to-end in ~7 seconds total wall time.

## What's in here

| File                  | Purpose |
|-----------------------|---------|
| `crm_mssql.sql`       | MSSQL source schema. Uses `IDENTITY`, `DATETIME2`, `DATETIMEOFFSET`, `UNIQUEIDENTIFIER`+`NEWID()`, `NVARCHAR(MAX)`, `BIT`, `AS (...) PERSISTED` computed columns, `GETUTCDATE()`/`SYSDATETIMEOFFSET()` defaults. |
| `crm_postgres.sql`    | PostgreSQL source schema. Uses `GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY`, `TIMESTAMPTZ`, `UUID`+`gen_random_uuid()`, `TEXT`, `JSONB`, `TEXT[]` arrays, `BOOLEAN`, `GENERATED ALWAYS AS (...) STORED`, regex `CHECK` predicates. |
| `crm_mysql.sql`       | MySQL 8.0+ / MariaDB 10.6+ source schema. Uses `AUTO_INCREMENT`, `DATETIME(6)`, `TIMESTAMP ... ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`, `ENUM`, `SET`, `JSON`, `GENERATED ... STORED`/`VIRTUAL`, `TINYINT(1)` booleans. |
| `run_matrix.sh`       | Drives uvg through all 9 permutations; reports per-pair counts and apply status. In strict/verification mode it also runs the MSSQL→PG column verifier. |
| `verify_columns.sh`   | Column-level metadata equivalence harness — used to spot-check fidelity (max_length, precision/scale, nullability, identity, TZ class, default presence). MSSQL→PG only; computed-column equivalence is a known limit. |

All three sources are 14 logical tables in the same shape (companies →
departments → employees → customers → orders → invoices → payments,
plus tags/junctions). Same conceptual schema, different dialects. This
gives uvg a wide test surface for type translation, default-expression
mapping, identifier quoting, CHECK predicate handling, and identity/
auto-increment semantics — without sprawling across many fixtures.

## Loading the source schemas

Assumes Docker containers `mssql-test` (port 1433), `pg-test` (port 5432),
`mysql-test` (port 3306) with password `TestPass2024`. Container names are
overridable via `$MSSQL_CONTAINER`, `$PG_CONTAINER`, `$MYSQL_CONTAINER`.

Also assumes `sqlcmd` on the **host** PATH (the runner shells out for
MSSQL targets — PG/MySQL go through `docker exec`). Microsoft's
[`go-sqlcmd`](https://github.com/microsoft/go-sqlcmd/releases) is a
single static binary that satisfies this — drop it in `~/.local/bin/`.

### MSSQL (azure-sql-edge or mssql-server)

```bash
# Create database
sqlcmd -S localhost,1433 -U sa -P TestPass2024 -C \
    -Q "IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE name='CRM_MSSQL') CREATE DATABASE CRM_MSSQL;"

# Load schema
sqlcmd -S localhost,1433 -U sa -P TestPass2024 -C -d CRM_MSSQL \
    -i testdata/crm/crm_mssql.sql
```

The MSSQL container's self-signed TLS cert may trip Go-based clients with
`x509: negative serial number`. The runner sets `GODEBUG=x509negativeserial=1`
to work around it; do the same when loading manually if needed.

### PostgreSQL

```bash
PGPASSWORD=TestPass2024 docker exec pg-test createdb -U postgres crm_pg
PGPASSWORD=TestPass2024 docker exec -i pg-test psql -U postgres -d crm_pg \
    < testdata/crm/crm_postgres.sql
```

### MySQL

```bash
docker exec mysql-test mysql -uroot -pTestPass2024 \
    -e "CREATE DATABASE crm_mysql;"
docker exec -i mysql-test mysql -uroot -pTestPass2024 crm_mysql \
    < testdata/crm/crm_mysql.sql
```

## Running the matrix

```bash
# Build a release binary (the runner prefers it).
cargo build --release

# Run all 9 (source, target) permutations.
./testdata/crm/run_matrix.sh

# Run all 9 permutations plus the MSSQL→PG column metadata verifier.
./testdata/crm/run_matrix.sh --verify-columns

# CI mode: exit non-zero if ANY pair is not OK, or if the MSSQL→PG
# column metadata verifier fails.
./testdata/crm/run_matrix.sh --strict
```

The same matrix also runs in CI via [`.github/workflows/matrix.yml`](../../.github/workflows/matrix.yml) on every PR, every push to `master`, and on a nightly cron — see the **Matrix** badge at the top of the repo README for status.

### No `sqlcmd` on host?

The runner expects Microsoft's `sqlcmd` on the host `PATH` for MSSQL target operations. If you'd rather not install it (CI does this too), use the included docker-exec shim:

```bash
sudo install -m 0755 testdata/crm/sqlcmd-shim.sh /usr/local/bin/sqlcmd
```

It proxies every `sqlcmd ...` invocation into the `mssql-test` container's bundled binary, so the runner works unchanged.

Output (truncated):

```
=== mssql -> mssql ===
  14    22    31    13    0s    OK
=== mssql -> postgres ===
  14    22    31    27    0s    OK
=== mssql -> mysql ===
  14    22    31    28    0s    OK
=== postgres -> mssql ===
  14    22    24    13    0s    OK
=== postgres -> postgres ===
  14    22    33    15    0s    OK
=== postgres -> mysql ===
  14    22    24    28    1s    OK
=== mysql -> mssql ===
  14    22    24    28    0s    OK
=== mysql -> postgres ===
  14    22    25    28    1s    OK
=== mysql -> mysql ===
  14    22    36    28    1s    OK

Summary: 9/9 OK, 0/9 failed
```

Per-pair logs and the generated DDL files land in `/tmp/uvg-matrix/`.
When column verification is enabled, the summary lands in
`/tmp/uvg-matrix/verify_columns.tsv` and the detailed log lands in
`/tmp/uvg-matrix/mssql_to_postgres.columns.log`.

## Expected counts

| pair          | tables | FKs | CHECKs | indexes |
|---------------|-------:|----:|-------:|--------:|
| mssql→mssql   | 14     | 22  | 31     | 13      |
| mssql→pg      | 14     | 22  | 31     | 27      |
| mssql→mysql   | 14     | 22  | 31     | 28      |
| pg→mssql      | 14     | 22  | 24     | 13      |
| pg→pg         | 14     | 22  | 33     | 15      |
| pg→mysql      | 14     | 22  | 24     | 28      |
| mysql→mssql   | 14     | 22  | 24     | 28      |
| mysql→pg      | 14     | 22  | 25     | 28      |
| mysql→mysql   | 14     | 22  | 36     | 28      |

Index counts vary because each engine creates implicit indexes on
unique constraints and foreign keys differently. CHECK counts vary
because some CHECKs are non-portable (regex predicates, `ARRAY[...]`
literals, `IN (0,1)` boolean ranges) and uvg drops them with a comment
on cross-dialect runs rather than emit DDL that fails to apply. The
3 same-dialect pairs preserve all source CHECKs verbatim.

## Column-level harness (`verify_columns.sh`)

By default, `run_matrix.sh` checks counts and apply status. With
`--strict` or `--verify-columns`, it also runs `verify_columns.sh` for
MSSQL→PG after applying `/tmp/uvg-matrix/mssql_to_postgres.sql`.

For deep fidelity verification on MSSQL→PG specifically,
`verify_columns.sh` extracts every column from both source and target and
compares per-attribute (max_length, precision/scale, nullability,
identity, TZ class, default presence).

```bash
./testdata/crm/verify_columns.sh CRM_MSSQL dbo uvg_matrix_mssql_to_postgres public
./testdata/crm/verify_columns.sh CRM_MSSQL dbo uvg_matrix_mssql_to_postgres public Companies
```

Known limits: computed-column presence/storage class and full
default-expression equivalence are not normalized yet. The other 8 pairs
still rely on count/apply checks; adding dialect-specific column
normalizers for those pairs is a follow-up rather than overfitting the
first gate.

## When to update the fixture

- New cross-dialect features land that should be exercised.
- A real customer schema reveals a translation gap; trim it down to
  reproduce in the CRM shape and add to all three dialect files.
- Add new tables conservatively — the CRM shape is intentionally a
  representative sample, not exhaustive coverage.

When changing the fixture, rerun `run_matrix.sh` and update the
expected-counts table in this README to match.
